core_mechanism: A variational maximum principle for the steady state of a mutation-selection (replicator-type) dynamical system, obtained by mapping the nonlinear dynamics onto an analytically solvable field theory: the equilibrium mean growth rate is fixed by extremizing a functional over configurations (equivalently the dominant eigenvalue of the associated linear operator), and the system undergoes sharp phase transitions between distinct steady-state phases as parameters are varied.
brief_reason: The abstract solves nonlinear generalizations of the model 'through an analytic field theoretic mapping' and gives 'exact analytical formulas for the equilibrium mean fitness ... in terms of a maximum principle' that hold for 'any permutation invariant replication rate function,' with 'selected phases' and phase diagrams signalling the associated phase transitions.
